name: TensorRT
pricing_notes: Free with NVIDIA GPU; included in CUDA/cuDNN stack

performance.notes: 2-10x faster than baseline PyTorch on NVIDIA GPUs via graph fusion and FP16/INT8 precision

agent_readiness.notes: NVIDIA's high-performance deep learning inference optimizer and runtime. Compiles PyTorch/ONNX models into TRT engines with kernel fusion, layer optimization, FP16/INT8 quantization, and dynamic tensor cores. Engines are hardware-specific (not portable). TensorRT-LLM for optimized LLM inference (used by Triton). Best for production NVIDIA GPU inference requiring maximum throughput.

gotchas: Compiled engines are GPU-architecture specific — can't share between GPU models
Compilation is slow (minutes to hours); cache engines for production
Not all PyTorch ops supported — custom plugins needed for unsupported ops
Dynamic shapes require explicit optimization profiles
best_when: Maximum inference throughput on NVIDIA GPUs (production serving)
FP16/INT8 precision reduction for faster inference with acceptable accuracy loss
LLM inference via TensorRT-LLM (used by major serving frameworks)
Computer vision pipelines on NVIDIA hardware requiring lowest latency
avoid_when: Non-NVIDIA hardware (use OpenVINO for Intel, Core ML for Apple)
Development/prototyping (overhead not worth it; use eager PyTorch)
summary: TensorRT is NVIDIA's high-performance deep learning inference optimizer. Compiles PyTorch/ONNX models into GPU-specific engines using kernel fusion, graph optimization, and FP16/INT8 quantization, achieving 2-10x speedup over baseline PyTorch. TensorRT-LLM extends this to LLMs. Best for production NVIDIA GPU serving requiring maximum inference throughput. Engines are hardware-specific and require recompilation.
